acceptance_tests_after_pi_approval: Every emitted object is a src.weather_model.WeatherMember.
Each primary year has 35040 quarter-hour rows, except leap years 2016 and 2020 with 35136 rows.
UTC timestamps are timezone-aware, strictly chronological, and spaced exactly 900 seconds.
Europe/Amsterdam local timestamps represent the same instants as the UTC axis.
temperature_c and pv_weather_fields.ghi_w_per_m2 have the same length as timestamps and contain only finite values.
For every KNMI source hour, the sum of four 15-minute GHI energy slices equals the source Q energy after unit conversion within floating tolerance.
Each member identity_record includes member_id, shared_weather_driver_id, source, cadence, content_sha256, provenance, metadata, and pv_weather_field_names.
HP and PV profiles generated from the same member pass assert_same_weather_realization on member/content identity.
PV seasonal totals and peak timing are checked against PVGIS/reference expectations before any integration use.
PVGIS TMY is rejected as a realized sampled weather path.

approval_questions.question: Approve UTC calendar-year weather members for the primary D-004 2014-2023 bundle, with Europe/Amsterdam local timestamps derived from each UTC instant?
approval_questions.rationale: The approved PVGIS request omits localtime and is therefore UTC-oriented; KNMI hourly slots are documented as UT; this avoids requiring extra boundary-year PVGIS retrieval before member construction.

approval_questions.question: Approve zero-order hourly-to-15-minute expansion for KNMI temperature, using the hourly observation value for the four quarter-hour instants in that hour?
approval_questions.recommendation: approve as a transparent first member-construction rule, with interpolation retained only as a later sensitivity if PI requests it

approval_questions.question: Approve energy-preserving conversion of KNMI hourly global radiation Q to realized GHI by converting J/cm2 per hour to hourly-average W/m2 and assigning that value to the four quarter-hour sub-intervals?
Approve PVGIS-SARAH3 seriescalc/TMY fields as calibration/validation provenance only, not as independently sampled realized weather paths?
approval_questions.recommendation: approve, preserving the existing D-004 source-use boundary

approval_questions.question: Approve one WeatherMember per year 2014-2023 with member IDs and shared driver IDs following this packet?

deferred_improvements.description: A later sensitivity may split each hourly KNMI Q value over four quarter-hour steps using solar-position, clear-sky, or PVGIS-derived within-hour weights, then renormalize the four values so their hourly integral exactly matches the KNMI source energy.
deferred_improvements.trigger: Revisit if PV timing, export peaks, or PV/HP simultaneity becomes decision-relevant.

metadata_outputs_after_pi_approval.raw_or_processed_member_storage: metadata-only unless PI approves ignored processed arrays under data/processed/weather_pv/
recorded_hashes: optional processed array sha256 if materialized

calendar.last_timestamp_utc_template: <YEAR>-12-31T23:45:00+00:00 for non-leap years; <YEAR>-12-31T23:45:00+00:00 after 35136 rows for leap years
calendar.local_timestamp_rule: derive each local timestamp by astimezone(Europe/Amsterdam) from the UTC timestamp

ghi_field.hourly_to_15min: repeat the hourly-average irradiance over the four quarter-hour sub-intervals so hourly radiation energy is preserved

knmi_hourly_source.hour_semantics: HH is UT; the source row with HH=h is treated as the hour-ending timestamp at h:00 UTC, with HH=24 mapping to 00:00 UTC on the following date

temperature_field.hourly_to_15min: repeat the hourly observation value over the four quarter-hour timestamps within the represented UTC hour

remaining_blockers: member builder implementation and focused real-source member tests remain pending
PVGIS seasonal/peak acceptance tolerances remain unsigned
